name: Quicky Crumble
recipeIngredient: 1 Granny Smith or Macintosh apple, cored
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure as much cereal as you want in your bowl (I use about 2 cups). Crush to fine crumbs in a blender, or place in a plastic bag, hold it shut, and crush with the heel of your hand.
2. Pour into a medium mixing bowl.
3. In a one-cup measure, microwave the butter on 100% power until melted; work the butter into the crumbs until the mixture is moist and crumbly.
4. Slice the apple into thin, even slices, then place in a microwave-safe, shallow baking dish.
5. Spread the cereal mixture evenly over the apple slices.
6. Microwave on 50% power for 3 minutes, or until heated through and the apple is tender.
7. Sprinkle with cinnamon and brown sugar; microwave on 50% power for an additional 2 minutes.
8. Serve hot with a little milk, a pat of butter or jam, or some fresh fruit.
Variations:
I have enjoyed this dish with banana slices in place of the apple. Also, substitute 1 tablespoon of your favorite jam for 1 tablespoon of the butter. Stir the jam into the butter in the cup immediately after the butter is melted. Try using an uncooked hot cereal, such as Cream of Wheat or Quaker Oat bran, instead of the Shreddies, for a deliciously different porridge. Serve with whipped cream, ice cream, or yogurt for dessert.
